- ## ![][image1] 

  # 

  # 

# Website Audit Checklist

- [ ] **Is your website loading quickly?**  
      - [ ] Go to your homepage on a mobile device and desktop—does everything above the fold completely load in under 3 seconds?  
      - [ ] Are your images optimized so the file sizes are as small as possible? If not, you can compress the size of your images using a site like ShortPixel (use the “glossy” setting)  
      - [ ] Are there any plug-ins you can remove from your site to increase speed?

      

- [ ] **Is your homepage optimized?**  
      - [ ] Are there words above the fold that communicate what you do, where you do it, and who you are?  
      - [ ] Control/command F search for “me,” “I,” or “we” and reword as many sentences as is appropriate to say “you” instead so your site is talking directly to your primary target market  
      - [ ] Are all fonts (especially above the fold) easy to read on both desktop and mobile?  
      - [ ] Does your “hero” image only take up 80-90% of the screen (maximum)? If it takes up more space than that, reduce size so anyone who visits your site will know there is more to it once you scroll  
      - [ ] Is there a clear call to action above the fold prompting the user to contact you?  
      - [ ] Do you have testimonials from past clients on your homepage? If not, add at least 2-3  
      - [ ] Scan your homepage in 30 seconds. Can you easily identify what services you offer, what location(s) you serve, and what value you might bring? If not, you will need to revise or restructure

- [ ] **Is your website easy to navigate?**  
      - [ ] If you click on your logo from any other page, does it redirect to the homepage?  
      - [ ] Can you easily & quickly identify where your navigation is on mobile & desktop?  
      - [ ] Check to see if your navigation is “sticky” on mobile & desktop—and, if not, change it so it is  
      - [ ] Do you have a clear, distinctive call to action button in your navigation that will take the user to your contact page?  
      - [ ] Use clear language in your navigation— including “pricing”/”packages” and “contact”  
      - [ ] Make sure all fonts are easily readable and no text is too big or too small. Break text up into smaller paragraphs, and properly format it with headings and subheadings (super important for SEO). If you skim just the headings, can you still identify the main message you are trying to communicate?  
          


- [ ] **Is your website mobile-optimized?**  
      - [ ] On your phone, go to your website—without scrolling, can you see your navigation, a call to action, and words that make it clear what services you offer?  
      - [ ] Now scroll through all of your main pages. Does your navigation stay? Are the text & fonts readable? Are there formatting issues? Can you skim the content easily?  
      - [ ] If you go to your contact page on desktop & mobile, is the form at least partially visible without having to scroll, and does it follow most common UX practices and have actual form fields to fill out (i.e. boxes)?

- [ ] **Is the purpose of your site clear?**  
      - [ ] Does your website include a homepage, a pricing page, a contact page, a portfolio, a blog, and an “about me/us” page?  
      - [ ] Do you communicate your “why” on your about page? Could a visitor to your site identify your mission, your values, and your beliefs?  
      - [ ] Remove any plagiarized sections—those clarify someone else’s “purpose,” not yours, and will ultimately feel ingenious and leave your clients with a disconnect between who you seem to be and who you actually are  
      - [ ] Control/command F search for “you” and “me” and reword as many sentences as is appropriate to say “you” instead of “me” or “I” so your site is talking directly to your primary target market  
      - [ ] Remove irrelevant items & services so your website is more specialized  
      - [ ] Remove information about your personal life that doesn’t motivate couples to inquire  
              
      - [ ] **Do you have social proof/reviews/ testimonials throughout your site?**  
      - [ ] Is all of your social proof on a “reviews” page? If so, redistribute this throughout your site  
      - [ ] Do you have reviews on your portfolio page that specifically talk about the experience of being photographed by you? If not, consider it\!  
      - [ ] Do you have reviews on your pricing page that specifically talk about how much value you provide? If not, consider it\!  
      - [ ] Do you have reviews on your about page that speak to who you are as a person? If not, consider it\!   
      - [ ] Count how many reviews you have on your site and per page—you should have 2-3 reviews minimum on each of your main pages  
      - [ ] If you have many reviews to pull from, read through each one and ask: does this showcase client success, how you helped them overcome challenges, and showcases what value you provide? If not, then prioritize putting a review that does on your site.  
              
- [ ] **Do you have calls to action (CTAs) everywhere on your site?**  
      - [ ] If you are CTA’ing to pages other than your contact form, make sure you are intentionally guiding the user through a journey  
      - [ ] If your CTA buttons blend in, modify them to have more contrast ☐ Go to your main pages (home, pricing, about, & contact) and count the number of folds/ scrolls. There should be at least 1 CTA per every 3 folds to try and keep users on your site for as long as possible (or until they complete the goal of filling out your contact form)  
      - [ ] Scroll to the bottom of each of your main pages and make sure you have a CTA

- [ ] **Have you reviewed your site with other people?**  
      - [ ] Find a fellow photographer or some non photographer friends and ask them to navigate your site on a mobile device. Ask them how quickly they can find your pricing information and to fill out your contact form  
      - [ ] Install Hotjar on your website and watch at least 10 recordings to see how others interact with your website to identify any user experience issues

- [ ] **Is your inquiry page and form optimized?**  
      - [ ] Does your form load at the same time as the rest of this page on mobile & desktop?  
      - [ ] Is the contact form visible above the fold on mobile & desktop?  
      - [ ] Is your form taking up the full width of the inquiry page? If so, reduce it so it does not (feels less overwhelming to someone filling it out)  
      - [ ] Do the questions in your form take up the full width of the form? If so, consider trying to restructure it so shorter questions are stacked in two columns  
      - [ ] Do you have the titles for each question above it—or do you only have the questions in the answer boxes (which is overwritten when the person starts typing)? If the question disappears when you start typing, restructure the form so that doesn’t happen to prevent confusion  
      - [ ] Is each question on your form essential to your initial inquiry response or data tracking? If not, remove it  
      - [ ] Did you ask for two forms of contact (in case they mistype)?  
      - [ ] Do you have a question asking for a general when & where?  
      - [ ] What does the final button say? If it says “submit,” consider changing it to a different word with a more positive connotation  
      - [ ] Does your contact form automatically redirect to your thank-you page?  
      - [ ] Test out your contact form to make sure it is easy to fill out and that you receive the response on your end—and if you are actively running ads, try to repeat this step once a week  
      - [ ] Ask a fellow photographer or friend to fill out and submit your contact form to make sure there are no glaring issues you are missing

- [ ] **Is your thank-you page that the couple is redirected to after filling out your contact form optimized?**  
      - [ ] Is your “thank you” response from your contact form on a completely separate page?  
      - [ ] Does it communicate excitement, next steps, and expectations?  
      - [ ] Does it remind your couples to check their spam or promotions?  
      - [ ] At the end of the form, have you given them something to do in the meantime?  
      - [ ] Does it load properly on desktop & mobile?

- [ ] **Is your packages & pricing page optimized?**  
      - [ ] Go to your pricing page on a mobile device and desktop—does everything above the fold completely load in under 3 seconds?  
      - [ ] Have you fully thought through how much pricing information you have available (full, starting/average, none)?  
      - [ ] Can your packages & pricing page be easily found in your navigation?  
      - [ ] Control/command F search for “me,” “I,” or “we” and reword as many sentences as is appropriate to say “you” instead so your site is talking directly to your primary target market  
      - [ ] Does this page position you as the solution to any problems that your target market might be facing?   
      - [ ] If you pricing is at the top of the page, move it down so you give yourself the chance to communicate your value first  
      - [ ] Have you communicated your value on this page to the absolute best of your ability, in a very clear way and listed out at some point?  
      - [ ] Does your primary header for the page mention something about pricing or packages?  
      - [ ] Do you have reviews on your pricing page that specifically talk about how much value you provide? If not, consider it\!  
      - [ ] Does this page address any objections that could keep a potential client from inquiring with you?  
      - [ ] If your pricing is laid out horizontally, change it so your packages are listed vertically so they have to scroll between them  
      - [ ] Are your packages listed from most expensive at the top to least expensive at the bottom?  
      - [ ] Do you have an FAQ at the bottom of the page that could address any lingering questions that might be holding back a couple from contacting you?  
      - [ ] Watch at least 10 recordings to see how others interact with your pricing page to identify any user experience or length issues

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
